fixture.description: On a hot dispatch path, resolve() throws and catches NoSuchMethodException on every superclass step for every event -> exceptions used as normal control flow, paying repeated stack-fill cost.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report identifies that resolve() throws and catches NoSuchMethodException on each class of the hierarchy as ordinary control flow, executed for every dispatch on the hot path (dispatch is documented as called thousands of times/second), so the hot path repeatedly pays exception-construction / stack-fill cost — and recommends a non-throwing lookup (e.g. iterate getDeclaredMethods() and match, or getMethod with a guard) or caching resolved methods including misses. Severity DEBT, SUGGESTED, or IMPORTANT. The finding may come from the performance agent. FAIL if the per-call-exception performance cost is not surfaced (e.g. the report only flags correctness or the empty catch body, but never the exception-as-control-flow cost on the hot path).

expected_behavior: Performance agent flags exceptions thrown/caught as control flow on a hot dispatch path
Recommends a non-throwing lookup or caching hits and misses
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT flag the documented catch as an error-handling bug (it is intentional control flow here)
